title: Stuffed Grape Leaves
description: A traditional Greek dish made with grape leaves stuffed with a delicious mixture of rice, meat, and spices.
instructions: Rinse the grape leaves and remove the stems.
Combine the cooked rice, chopped stuffed grape leaves, ground lamb, and Greek seasoning in a bowl.
Mix well and season with salt to taste.
Place a grape leaf on a flat surface, with the stem end facing you.
Place a tablespoon of the rice mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining grape leaves and filling.
Place the stuffed grape leaves seam-side down in a large saucepan or Dutch oven.
Add enough water to the saucepan to cover the leaves.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the leaves are tender.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
